intro: Cosmea tenella, also known as Indian cock'scomb, is an annual herb native to tropical Asia and Africa, characterized by its bright red, hairy flowers and leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Cosmea tenella include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Cosmea tenella is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
